steps.title: Clear the denominators
steps.content: Factor the denominator as $x^2-9=(x+3)(x-3)$. Multiplying the equation by $(x+3)(x-3)$ gives $$4x^2-2x(x-3)=x+3$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content: Expand and move everything to one side: $$4x^2-2x^2+6x=x+3$$ $$2x^2+5x-3=0$$ $$(2x-1)(x+3)=0$$

steps.title: Reject the undefined value
steps.content: The possible values are $x=\frac{1}{2}$ and $x=-3$. But $x=-3$ makes a denominator equal to $0$ in the original equation, so it is not allowed. The answer is C.
